title: Boyer-Moore majority vote: O(n) time, O(1) space, correct
statement_informal: The Boyer-Moore majority vote finds a strict majority element in one left-to-right pass using only a candidate and a counter (O(1) space). Correctness: if some m occurs in more than half the list (length l < 2*cnt m l) then the final candidate equals m. Proved via the cancellation invariant.

cost_model.notes: One fold_left over the list; each step does O(1) work with a single (candidate,count) pair. The correctness invariant is (A) forall x<>c, 2*cnt x prefix + count <= length prefix; (B) 2*cnt c prefix <= length prefix + count, maintained by rev_ind.

references: R. Boyer and J. Moore, 'MJRTY — A Fast Majority Vote Algorithm' (1981/1991).
The O(1)-space streaming majority element; the verify-track companion to the average/distribution units on selection.
